    • NNDM
    • Prediction-domain adaptive evaluation
    • TWCV

PDAV

Prediction-domain adaptive validation methods provide estimates of map accuracy that are adapted to the deployment or prediction domain (Linnenbrink, Nowosad & Meyer, 2026).

The package aims to:

  1. provide consistent implementations of prediction-domain adaptive validation methods for spatial predictive modelling;
  2. provide vignettes that describe how the methods work and how they can be applied;
  3. provide an overview of currently available methods.

Available methods

Method Main idea Reference Documentation
NNDM Leave-one-out nearest-neighbour distance matching. Matches the nearest-neighbour distance distribution encountered during prediction by excluding training points from the LOO CV. Milà et al. (2022) Function, Article
kNNDM k-fold nearest-neighbour distance matching. Uses clustering to create fold configurations ranging from random to spatially clustered CV and selects the configuration that best approximates the prediction situation. Linnenbrink & Milà et al. (2024) Function, Article
DA-CV Uses adversarial validation to classify the prediction area into (dis-)similar to the training data, and then weights random and spatial CV results based on the proportion of the two areas. Wang et al. (2025) Function, Article
TWCV Uses raking to weight the error estimates at the training points to match the frequency distribution of the predictor variables at the prediction locations. Brenning & Suesse (2026) Function, Article

Detailed comparison

For a more detailed comparison of advantages, disadvantages, and assumptions, see the overview article.
